info.name: EduFlow Gateway + Auth Postman Collection
info.description:
Postman collection for the current EduFlow gateway, auth, and user flows.

Covers:
- API Gateway request authorization
- Auth service register, login, refresh, validate, roles, and permissions
- User service reads, updates, and instructor approval through the gateway

Highlights:
- Stores access token, refresh token, role, auth user ID, and permissions for each actor
- Tests permission-based role management with the current gateway contract
- Includes current self-access caveat caused by auth numeric IDs vs user-service route IDs

Run order:
1. Registration and login requests
2. Validate tokens
3. Role and permission management
4. User management and gateway security checks

item.name: Flow 1: Registration & Onboarding
item.description: Register users with different roles. Run these first to create test accounts.

exec: pm.test('All stored auth and role-management variables cleared', function() {

request.description: Utility request to clear all stored tokens before starting fresh.

exec: pm.test('Register returns 200 or user already exists', function() {
pm.test('Student register response carries tokens and permissions', function() {

request.description: Register a new student (defaults to STUDENT role). If user exists, use Login Student instead.

exec: pm.test('Register returns 200 or user already exists', function() {

request.description: Register a new instructor with INSTRUCTOR role.

exec: pm.test('Register returns 200 or user already exists', function() {

request.description: Register a new admin with ADMIN role. Note: Admin role assignment may require existing admin privileges depending on your auth-service configuration.

item.name: Flow 2: Authentication Core
item.description: Login, refresh tokens, and validate JWT tokens for all roles.

exec: pm.test('Auth response includes permissions array', function() {

request.description: Login as student and store tokens automatically.

exec: pm.test('Auth response includes permissions array', function() {

request.description: Login as instructor and store tokens automatically.

exec: pm.test('Auth response includes permissions array', function() {
pm.test('Admin token carries role-management permission', function() {

request.description: Login as admin and store tokens automatically.

exec: pm.test('Token validation response includes permissions', function() {
pm.test('Token is valid and has STUDENT role', function() {

request.description: Validate student access token and store user ID for downstream requests.
Validate instructor access token.

exec: pm.test('Admin validation includes role-management permission', function() {

request.description: Validate admin access token.

exec: pm.test('Refresh returns a new access token and permissions array', function() {

request.description: Refresh student access token using refresh token.

exec: pm.test('Refresh returns a new access token and permissions array', function() {

request.description: Refresh instructor access token.

exec: pm.test('Refresh returns a new access token and permissions array', function() {

request.description: Refresh admin access token.

item.name: Flow 3: Role & Permission Management
item.description: Manage roles and permissions through the gateway. These requests require a token carrying the roles:manage permission.

exec: pm.test('Get all roles returns 200 for roles:manage token', function() {

request.description: Get all roles. Requires roles:manage.

exec: pm.test('Role response has required fields', function() {

request.description: Create a new role. Requires roles:manage.

exec: pm.test('Get role by name returns 200', function() {

request.description: Get a role by name. Requires roles:manage.

exec: pm.test('Response is an array of permissions', function() {

request.description: List all permissions. Requires roles:manage.
Create a new permission. Requires roles:manage.

exec: pm.test('Role now includes test permission', function() {

request.description: Add permissions to a role using the current array-body contract. Requires roles:manage.

exec: pm.test('Role no longer includes test permission', function() {

request.description: Remove a permission from the test role. Requires roles:manage.
Assign a role to a user. Requires roles:manage. Uses studentAuthUserId from token validation.
Delete the test role. Requires roles:manage.

item.name: Flow 4: User Management
item.description: User profile management via user-service. Tests RBAC for all roles.

exec: pm.test('Admin can list users - returns 200', function() {
pm.test('Response is an array of users', function() {

request.description: List all users. Requires users:read. Also harvests user IDs for subsequent tests.

exec: pm.test('Admin can get user by ID', function() {

request.description: Get user by ID. ADMIN only. Run 'Admin: List All Users' first.

exec: pm.test('Admin can lookup user by email', function() {
pm.test('User response has correct email', function() {

request.description: Get user by email. ADMIN only.
Update user profile. ADMIN only.
Approve an instructor. ADMIN only. Run 'Admin: List All Users' first to populate instructorRouteId.

exec: pm.test('Student self-read passes gateway RBAC', function() {
pm.test('Student self-read is NOT 401 or 403', function() {

request.description: Student reading own profile. Gateway should allow (self-access). Note: May return 400 due to ID mismatch between auth-service (numeric) and user-service (UUID).

exec: pm.test('Student self-update passes gateway RBAC', function() {
pm.test('Student self-update is NOT 401 or 403', function() {

request.description: Student updating own profile.

exec: pm.test('Student cross-user read is forbidden (403)', function() {

request.description: Student trying to read another user's profile. Should return 403.

exec: pm.test('Student cross-user update is forbidden (403)', function() {

request.description: Student trying to update another user's profile. Should return 403.
Student trying to list all users. Should return 403.

exec: pm.test('Student email lookup is forbidden (403)', function() {

request.description: Student trying to lookup user by email. Should return 403.
Student trying to approve an instructor. Should return 403.

exec: pm.test('Instructor self-read passes gateway RBAC', function() {
pm.test('Instructor self-read is NOT 401 or 403', function() {

request.description: Instructor reading own profile.
Instructor trying to list all users. Should return 403.
Instructor trying to approve another instructor. Should return 403.

item.name: Flow 5: Gateway & Security Verification
item.description: Test API Gateway security: public paths, authentication enforcement, RBAC at gateway level.

exec: pm.test('Public login endpoint is reachable without token', function() {

request.description: Verify /api/auth/** is public and does not require Authorization header.

exec: pm.test('Public register endpoint is reachable without token', function() {

request.description: Verify register endpoint is public.

item.name: Public Endpoint: Course Read Without Token

exec: pm.test('Public course GET bypasses auth', function() {
pm.test('Course service may not be running (expected 404/500)', function() {

request.description: Verify GET /api/courses/** is public. 404/500 expected if course-service is not running.
Verify gateway rejects protected routes when no Authorization header is supplied.
Verify malformed/invalid JWT tokens are rejected with 403.
Verify empty Bearer token is rejected.

exec: pm.test('Request passes gateway with valid token', function() {
pm.test('Gateway should add X-User-Id, X-User-Email, X-User-Role headers', function() {
console.log('Check your service logs for mutated headers: X-User-Id, X-User-Email, X-User-Role');

request.description: Verify gateway mutates request with X-User-Id, X-User-Email, X-User-Role headers. Check downstream service logs.

item.name: Flow 6: JWT Edge Cases & Negative Tests
item.description: Test JWT security: expired tokens, tampered signatures, missing claims, malformed tokens.

request.description: Test with an expired JWT token. Should return 403.
Test with JWT that has valid structure but tampered signature. Should return 403.
Test with completely malformed JWT (not 3 base64 segments). Should return 403.
Test with valid JWT but missing 'Bearer ' prefix. Should return 401 or 403.

exec: pm.test('Token with missing claims should be rejected', function() {

request.description: Test with JWT that has missing required claims (no role, no email). Should be rejected.

exec: pm.test('Algorithm confusion attack should be rejected', function() {

request.description: Test algorithm confusion attack (alg: none). Should be rejected by gateway.
